discussion_comments.comment_body: better convention here would be to return (string, string, error).  the error case can then return "", "", fmt.Errorf(...).  that feels more go-like than returning the remote name of "error"

currently no way to display errors
I'm thinking add an error in the chat message type and then check for it in the client

this uses the index of the chat message in the chat message array to update messages in order to enable streaming

the caller needs to know this and call ScreenMemGetCmdInfoMessageCount and use that as the message id when creating a message
maybe instead of doing the log.Printf, have this func return an `error`.
